relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The altar is shaped square from acacia wood.
Bronze covers the altar and its horns.
Bronze tools serve the altar's tending.
A bronze network receives rings.
The grating rests beneath the ledge.
Bronze-covered poles make the altar portable.
The altar can be carried through the journey.
The mountain pattern shapes even the hollow boards.
The court begins with linen hangings southward.
Bronze bases and silver hooks hold the court.
The north side mirrors ordered support.
The west side closes the court.
The east side faces sunrise.
One shoulder of the gate is measured.
The second shoulder is measured.
The gate screen carries sacred colors.
Silver and bronze bind the court together.
The court receives length, width, height, and linen.
Even pegs belong to sacred service.
Pure beaten olive oil keeps light rising continually.
Aaron and his sons tend the lamp from evening to morning before YHWH.

contemplative_note: Exodus 27 gives the altar, court, and continual lamp: the holy place has boundaries, tools, entrance, and light that must be tended.
